document.title: Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits
document.text:
# Demo Paper: CappinCheck for Agentic Claim Audits

## Abstract

We introduce CappinCheck, an agentic workflow that improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks. The system robustly generalizes across domains and is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.

## Results

We compare CappinCheck against a baseline claim-review pipeline on Benchmark X. The baseline system achieves 84.1% on the benchmark. CappinCheck achieves 87.3% on the same benchmark.

| System | Benchmark X accuracy |
| --- | ---: |
| Baseline | 84.1% |
| CappinCheck | 87.3% |

## Discussion

The benchmark contains curated technical claims from public documents. We did not evaluate prospective real-world deployment, clinical documents, legal documents, or private enterprise workflows. We did not run an external literature search for prior scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems.

claims.claim: Our method improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claims.why_risky: The claim combines a large numeric improvement with broad real-world language.
claims.audit_question: Do the reported benchmark numbers justify a 30% improvement and real-world generalization?

claims.claim: The system robustly generalizes across domains.
claims.why_risky: Generalization language is stronger than typical benchmark evidence.
claims.audit_question: Were multiple domains and out-of-distribution settings actually tested?

claims.claim: This is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.
claims.why_risky: First/novelty claims require broad literature comparison.
claims.audit_question: Does prior work include similar scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems?

claim.claim: Our method improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.
claim.why_risky: The claim combines a large numeric improvement with broad real-world language.
claim.audit_question: Do the reported benchmark numbers justify a 30% improvement and real-world generalization?

audits.why: The reference supports a narrower benchmark improvement, but not a 30% improvement on real-world tasks.
audits.weaker_supported_rewrite: CappinCheck improves from 84.1% to 87.3% on Benchmark X, a 3.2 percentage-point gain and 3.8% relative improvement under the benchmark conditions.

supporting_evidence.relevance: The table supports a 3.2-point absolute gain, not a 30% relative improvement.

missing_context: No real-world deployment task is reported in the demo document.

contrast.claim_text: Our method improves performance by 30% over prior work on real-world tasks.

reference_sources.title: Benchmark X Report
reference_sources.why_relevant: Contains the baseline and CappinCheck benchmark scores used by the claim.

best_sources.title: Benchmark X Report
best_sources.evidence_summary: The reference reports 84.1% for the baseline and 87.3% for CappinCheck on Benchmark X.
best_sources.key_qualification: The evidence is one curated benchmark, not real-world tasks; the computed gain is 3.2 percentage points and 3.8% relative.

contrast.delta_explanation: The reference supports a narrower benchmark improvement, but not a 30% improvement on real-world tasks.
contrast.suggested_rewrite: CappinCheck improves from 84.1% to 87.3% on Benchmark X, a 3.2 percentage-point gain and 3.8% relative improvement under the benchmark conditions.

claim.claim: The system robustly generalizes across domains.
claim.why_risky: Generalization language is stronger than typical benchmark evidence.
claim.audit_question: Were multiple domains and out-of-distribution settings actually tested?

audits.why: The claim generalizes beyond the reference, which only supports performance on curated Benchmark X.
audits.weaker_supported_rewrite: CappinCheck generalizes across the curated domains represented in Benchmark X; real-world deployment performance was not evaluated.
missing_context: Additional external grounding would be needed for a production verdict.

contrast.claim_text: The system robustly generalizes across domains.

reference_sources.title: Benchmark X Report
reference_sources.why_relevant: Defines the evaluation setting and its limitations.

best_sources.title: Benchmark X Report
best_sources.evidence_summary: The reference describes curated technical claims from public documents.
best_sources.key_qualification: It does not evaluate clinical, legal, private-enterprise, or real-world deployment settings.

contrast.delta_explanation: The claim generalizes beyond the reference, which only supports performance on curated Benchmark X.
contrast.suggested_rewrite: CappinCheck generalizes across the curated domains represented in Benchmark X; real-world deployment performance was not evaluated.

claim.claim: This is the first agentic workflow for scientific claim auditing.
claim.why_risky: First/novelty claims require broad literature comparison.
claim.audit_question: Does prior work include similar scientific fact-checking or claim-audit systems?

audits.why: The claim may be plausible, but the demo document does not provide enough scope or external evidence.
audits.weaker_supported_rewrite: The document provides preliminary evidence for a narrower version of this claim.
missing_context: Additional external grounding would be needed for a production verdict.
